fields.description:
## Expected Behavior
When the review workflow dispatches a code reviewer sub-agent, it must use one of the named dso:code-reviewer-* agents (light/standard/deep/security/performance/test-quality tiers) as specified in CLAUDE.md. The classifier selects the tier; the named agent is dispatched via the Agent tool with the agent file read verbatim.

## Actual Behavior
In long-running sessions (particularly after context compaction), the orchestrator dispatches a generic general-purpose sub-agent for code review instead of the named agent. This causes reviews to use incorrect prompts, skip tier selection logic, and produce findings that do not conform to the reviewer-findings.json contract. The pre-commit review gate may still pass because the hash matches, but the review quality is degraded.

## Root Cause (suspected)
Instruction Locality: the mandate to use named dso:code-reviewer-* agents is documented in CLAUDE.md but the REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md execution site lacks a sufficiently prominent co-located reminder. After context compaction, the CLAUDE.md rule drifts out of working memory.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Start a long debug/sprint session
2. Allow context compaction to occur
3. Invoke /dso:review or reach the review step in /dso:commit
4. Observe the Agent tool call — check if subagent_type is 'general-purpose' instead of 'dso:code-reviewer-*'

## Affected Files
- plugins/dso/docs/workflows/REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md (dispatch step)
- CLAUDE.md (named agent mandate)
